ops.title: Background/workflow subagents hang indefinitely when a tool call needs interactive permission — scope their prompts to pre-authorized operations
ops.body:
A workflow subagent whose Bash call triggers a permission prompt (e.g. `rm -rf` under `~/.claude/state`) hangs forever: nothing can answer the prompt, the agent shows "running" with no progress, and the whole workflow's barrier never resolves. One such call wedged a 27-agent battery for ~2 hours (2026-07-04); killing the workflow surfaced as "User rejected tool use" in the agent transcript.

**Why:** Background agents have no interactive channel; an ask-rule match parks the tool call in a queue nobody drains.

**How to apply:** When authoring agent/workflow prompts, keep destructive-looking commands out of scratch flows (write to fresh temp dirs instead of deleting under `~/.claude` or other protected paths). If a background agent shows no progress for many minutes but its last transcript entry is a pending Bash call, suspect a permission prompt — kill and re-scope rather than wait. Diagnose via the agent's transcript tail: a `tool_use` with no matching `tool_result` is the tell. Related: [[parallel-sessions-own-the-working-copy]].
